text.title: North Star Metric Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules grounding North Star Metric. Each rule pairs a binary statement, a rationale, and a source. Decision tree branches in 06-decision-tree.xml reference these ids.

rule.statement: Before running North Star Metric, every item in the AGENTS.md 'Applies If' list must hold; if any fails, the methodology must be skipped.

rationale.source: Sean Ellis — North Star Metric concept

rule.rationale: Methodologies executed outside their preconditions produce plausible-looking but invalid output. Running the gate prevents wasted cycles.

rule.statement: The producing agent MUST emit an artefact validating against content/02-output-contract.xml's JSON Schema; any field outside the schema is rejected.
rule.rationale: Hard schema is the only mechanical interlock between a synthesising LLM and downstream consumers; prose contracts get reinterpreted.

rule.statement: Every output produced by this methodology MUST name a single human owner with edit rights and a review deadline.
rule.rationale: Orphan artefacts rot; named ownership is the only durable mechanism for keeping the artefact current.

rule.statement: Any deviation from the canonical procedure MUST be logged with a one-line rationale and surfaced at the next retro.
rule.rationale: Undocumented deviations destroy institutional memory; PDCA requires a written delta.

rule.statement: Before any binding downstream action driven by this output (publish, send, rollout, commit), a named human reviewer MUST sign off.
rule.rationale: LLM outputs are first drafts; the human checkpoint is the safety gate against hallucinated commitments.

rule.statement: If 'Applies If' evaluates false OR 'Skip If' evaluates true, the agent MUST NOT run North Star Metric; it returns an action=skip envelope and yields control to the parent workflow.
rule.rationale: Explicit skip exit prevents agents from forcing the methodology into incompatible contexts.

section.title: Sources
section.reference: Sean Ellis — North Star Metric concept
